title: Discovery Service: Service Discovery API and Signed Service Descriptor Pattern
description: Implement the service discovery registry that allows consumer sentinels to resolve the current endpoint(s) for a target producer service. Endpoint data remains off-chain (volatile operational state). The producer sentinel signs a ServiceDescriptor document (JWS) and publishes it to Discovery. Consumer sentinels fetch the descriptor via a resolve API and verify the producer's signature before trusting the endpoint. Optionally, hash(descriptor) is anchored on-chain for tamper evidence.
objectives: Consumer sentinels can always resolve a current, producer-signed endpoint for any registered service
Descriptor signature verification ensures endpoint data was produced by the legitimate producer — not an attacker who compromised Discovery
TTL on descriptors prevents stale endpoint usage without requiring explicit invalidation

steps: Step 1 - ServiceDescriptor Schema: Define as a Pydantic model and JSON schema. Fields: { descriptor_id (UUID v4), service_id (str), env ('dev'|'test'|'prod'), producer_sentinel_did (str), producer_service_did (str), endpoints: [ { url (AnyHttpUrl), protocol ('http'|'https'|'grpc'|'mqtt'), weight (int 1-100), instance_id (str, optional) } ], valid_from (ISO-8601), valid_until (ISO-8601, max TTL 10 minutes), issued_at (ISO-8601) }. Max 10 endpoint entries per descriptor. url max 2048 chars.

Step 2 - Producer Publishes Descriptor: POST /api/v1/services/{service_id}/descriptor. Auth: sentinel bearer token for a producer sentinel registered for this service_id + env. Request body: { signed_descriptor_jws }. Validate: (a) JWS signature using producer_sentinel_did's public key (resolve from did:key or DID document), (b) service_id and env in descriptor match URL params, (c) producer_sentinel_did is registered in DB as an active producer sentinel for this service, (d) valid_until = issued_at + TTL ≤ 10 minutes max, (e) endpoints array not empty. On success: upsert into service_descriptors table (keyed by service_id + env). Write audit event.

Step 3 - ServiceDescriptor DB Storage: Table service_descriptors: { id (UUID PK), service_id (TEXT NOT NULL), env (TEXT NOT NULL), producer_sentinel_did (TEXT), descriptor_hash (TEXT — SHA-256 of canonical descriptor JSON), signed_descriptor_jws (TEXT NOT NULL), valid_until (TIMESTAMPTZ), published_at (TIMESTAMPTZ default now()), is_active (BOOL default true) }. UNIQUE(service_id, env). Keep last 5 versions in service_descriptor_history for audit. Index on (service_id, env, is_active).

Step 4 - Consumer Resolve API: GET /api/v1/registry/resolve?service_id=billing-api&env=prod. Auth: sentinel bearer token. Returns: { service_id, env, signed_descriptor_jws, descriptor_hash, valid_until, producer_sentinel_did }. Consumer must: (a) verify JWS signature using producer_sentinel_did, (b) check valid_until > now(), (c) optionally verify descriptor_hash matches on-chain hash in ServiceRegistry. If no active descriptor → 404 SERVICE_NOT_RESOLVABLE.

Step 5 - Descriptor Expiry Enforcement: Do NOT return descriptors where valid_until < now(). If expired: return 404 with { code: 'DESCRIPTOR_EXPIRED', message: 'No valid service descriptor. Producer sentinel may be offline.' }. Discovery does NOT auto-renew descriptors — the producer sentinel must re-publish proactively (TASK-040).

Step 6 - On-Chain Hash Anchoring (Optional): If ANCHOR_DESCRIPTORS_ON_CHAIN=true: after accepting a new descriptor, call ServiceRegistryClient.update_descriptor_hash(service_id, env, hash) in background task. Store tx_hash in service_descriptors. Consumer can verify: resolve on-chain hash from ServiceRegistry and compare against received descriptor_hash.

Step 7 - Descriptor Broadcasting: On new descriptor publish: emit an internal event (Redis pub/sub or in-process). Any active long-poll requests or SSE streams on GET /api/v1/registry/events?service_id=billing-api receive a notification. This allows consumer sentinels to react immediately to endpoint changes rather than waiting for next poll.

Step 8 - Multi-Endpoint Selection Hints: The descriptor endpoints array includes weight fields. Discovery does not perform load balancing — it returns all endpoints weighted. Consumer sentinel selects based on weight (or round-robin) and its own health state. Instance_id allows consumer to pin to a specific instance if desired.

Step 9 - Admin Inspect Descriptor: GET /api/v1/services/{service_id}/descriptor (admin only). Returns current descriptor metadata + JWS for admin inspection. Does NOT bypass signature verification requirement in consumer path.

Step 10 - Validation Strictness: Descriptor published with producer_sentinel_did that does not match DB sentinel record for service_id+env → 403 UNAUTHORIZED_PUBLISHER. Descriptor with valid_until more than 10 minutes in future → 422 TTL_TOO_LONG. Descriptor with 0 endpoints → 422 EMPTY_ENDPOINTS.

error_handling.message: No active service descriptor found for 'billing-api' in env 'prod'
Service descriptor has expired. Producer sentinel may be offline.

error_handling.condition: Publisher DID does not match registered producer
error_handling.message: producer_sentinel_did does not match the registered producer for this service
JWS signature verification failed for service descriptor

security_constraints: Consumer sentinel MUST verify JWS signature using producer_sentinel_did before trusting any endpoint in the descriptor
Discovery only stores and forwards descriptors — it does not add, modify, or re-sign them
producer_sentinel_did authenticated against registered sentinel in DB — arbitrary DIDs cannot publish
valid_until hard cap: 10 minutes — prevents long-lasting stale descriptors if producer goes offline
All descriptor publish events write to audit_events

acceptance_criteria: Producer sentinel publishes a signed descriptor → consumer sentinel resolves it and verifies signature
Descriptor with invalid signature is rejected with 400 INVALID_DESCRIPTOR_SIGNATURE
Descriptor published by a DID not matching the registered producer → 403 UNAUTHORIZED_PUBLISHER
On-chain hash anchoring stores tx_hash in DB after ServiceRegistry.update_descriptor_hash() call
